title: hs:shape experiment spec frontmatter
description: Shape of one EXP-<n>.md frontmatter block under docs/product/shape/experiments/, as written by experiment_spec.py / experiment_verdict.py (verification-tiering rule, kẹp 2 đầu). Backing for the author + verdict pair -- not a prose-only convention.

id.description: Parent-free, globally monotonic within the workspace experiments dir (max existing EXP-<n>.md filename + 1, never reused).

linked_to.description: brd_goal/prd/epic ids the experiment bears on (resolved against hs:spec's spec graph; a story id or a truly missing id both surface as dangling).

design.description: control/variant or method description; free-form, no code, no fetch instructions.

decision_rule.description: Same 3-tier ratio-floor shape as the 'Verdict math' paragraph in frontmatter-and-id-spec.md's Outcome records section (outcome_verdict.py itself is a module in the separate, out-of-skill product-spec project -- never imported here). Ordering `0 <= partial_floor < hit_floor <= 1` is enforced by experiment_spec.validate_decision_rule() at write time -- JSON Schema draft-07 has no cross-property relational keyword to express 'partial_floor < hit_floor' directly, so this schema only bounds each field to [0,1] individually.

status.description: experiment_spec.author() writes 'draft'; experiment_verdict.apply_verdict() flips it to 'concluded'. No third state is ever emitted.

measured_on.description: ISO 8601 date the verdict was applied; null until concluded.

dangling_linked_to.description: Subset of linked_to that did not resolve to a goal/prd/epic node in hs:spec's spec graph at author time (flagged, not rejected).

verdict_actor.description: Attribution for the verdict pass specifically -- experiment_verdict.apply_verdict() writes this (distinct from the author-time `actor`) whenever an actor is supplied at conclude time.
